title: ImplementationReport
description: The implementer's structured return. Note what is NOT here: gate results. Gates are command activities with their own recorded exit statuses; the implementer never certifies them (rigid step 4).

summary.description: What was built, in complete sentences a reviewer can orient from.

change.description: One line: what changed here and why.

gate_id.description: Acceptance-gate id from the brief (G1, G2...).

how.description: The test/change that discharges it — by name, so the reviewer can go read it.

gates_addressed.description: Mapping from every brief acceptance gate to the work that discharges it. A gate with no entry means the implementer believes it is out of reach — say so in concerns, do not omit silently.

deviations.description: Every departure from the brief, however small. An undeclared deviation found in review is treated as a defect regardless of whether the code is right.

new_tests.description: Test names added, each asserting an outcome (see brief gate audit).

concerns.description: Anything the implementer is unsure about — the reviewer reads this FIRST. Empty concerns on a nontrivial diff is itself a signal.
